artifact_scope: Lane-scoped final-review blocker packet for sdxl_realvisxl_base_lane.
implementation_summary: Reviewed W63 target-runtime smoke, pullback, W63 visual QA, W69 single-hand contact close-up QA, W69 two-character hand-to-body QA, tracker mirrors, and runtime queue promotion rule without rerunning live runtime.

tests_performed.name: final_review_work_order_present
tests_performed.expected: Base lane final-certification-review work order is present

tests_performed.name: generic_target_runtime_smoke_exists
tests_performed.expected: W63 base target-runtime static proof and smoke exist and stopped EC2

tests_performed.name: runtime_visual_qa_scope_is_smoke_only

observed.decision: RealVisXL runtime smoke image QA passed. This proves the target runtime can generate and return a coherent image, but it is not final portfolio/style certification.

tests_performed.expected: W63 visual QA passes runtime smoke but explicitly excludes final certification

tests_performed.name: single_hand_local_qa_disallows_final

observed.reason: The sample passes the local single-hand tabletop-contact close-up target, but final RealVisXL certification requires broader portrait/full-image scope and target-runtime proof.

tests_performed.expected: single-hand local QA passes with notes but blocks final decision/promotion

tests_performed.name: two_character_local_qa_disallows_final

remaining_risks: This is one local preferred sample after one local QA-driven rerun, not multi-seed robustness.
This does not use the deterministic Wave25 contact mask as an inpaint/refine mask yet.
This does not prove target-runtime EC2 behavior.
This does not certify final multi-character contact, contact shadows, pixel-level ownership, or no-merge behavior across prompts.

tests_performed.expected: two-character local QA passes with notes but disallows final certification

tests_performed.name: queue_rule_requires_no_final_promotion_from_local_samples

observed.promotion_rule: Do not promote to final RealVisXL certification from the close-up or two-character local samples alone. They prove local single-hand tabletop-contact close-up quality and a first Wave25-linked two-character contact pixel attempt only; mask-routed refine, robustness, target-runtime proof, and final certification remain separate.

tests_performed.expected: runtime queue explicitly prevents final promotion from current local samples alone

qa_summary.runtime_visual_decision: RealVisXL runtime smoke image QA passed. This proves the target runtime can generate and return a coherent image, but it is not final portfolio/style certification.
runtime_visual_defects: Minor beauty-retouch smoothness in skin texture; acceptable for runtime smoke but not a final hyperreal quality bar by itself.
The close crop prevents evaluation of hands, body proportions, clothing, and background, so this proof should not be overextended beyond image-generation/runtime viability.
single_hand_remaining_notes: This certifies a local close-up contact target only; it does not certify portrait-with-hands composition, two-hand separation, full-body anatomy, or final RealVisXL image quality.
The wrist enters from the frame edge by design for the close-up composition, so full-arm anatomy is not scored.
two_character_remaining_risks: This is one local preferred sample after one local QA-driven rerun, not multi-seed robustness.
This does not use the deterministic Wave25 contact mask as an inpaint/refine mask yet.
This does not prove target-runtime EC2 behavior.
This does not certify final multi-character contact, contact shadows, pixel-level ownership, or no-merge behavior across prompts.

known_issues: W63 proves the base lane can run on target runtime and return a coherent close-face smoke image, but that QA explicitly says it is not final portfolio/style certification.
W69 single-hand evidence passes only an isolated tabletop-contact close-up and explicitly blocks final decision/promotion.
W69 two-character evidence is a first pixel-facing attempt after one QA-driven rerun, not multi-seed robustness or mask-routed refine.
The base lane runtime queue promotion rule explicitly says not to promote final RealVisXL certification from the current local samples alone.
This blocker packet does not consume candidate masks as truth, promote masks, rerun Wave70 hard gates, or activate Wave71+.
certification_boundary: Lane-scoped blocker review only. This does not certify full project completion, final RealVisXL base-lane quality, body-mask readiness, Wave70 mask promotion, or Wave71+ activation.
next_action: Do not close the base lane final-review work order from current evidence. Add mask-routed refine or a small robustness pair for the base contact scope, then obtain candidate-appropriate target-runtime proof before final-review closure.
